items.title: How often do the greats get traded? 🤔
items.author: Basketball Reference
items.body_excerpt:
How often do the greats get traded? 🤔

Giannis Antetokounmpo ranks 23rd all-time, among qualifying players, in win shares per 48 minutes. Of the top 25, more than half of them have been traded at least once.

Of the 14 who have been traded, the majority (8 out of 14) have been dealt more than once.

items.title: [Krawczynski] Edwards wants to win right now, and the Wolves are banking on addition by subtraction, believing that removing Randle’s ISO-heavy offensive game and redistributing his 15.3 shots per game to McDaniels and Reid will open things up for Minnesota in a different way.
items.author: urfaselol
items.body_excerpt: The Wolves could not afford to run it back again after making minimal changes to the group in the summer of 2025, especially after watching Towns win a championship in New York this season. Edwards wants to win right now, and the Wolves are banking on addition by subtraction,…

media.title: Heat Finally Land Giannis Antetokounmpo In Blockbuster Trade
media.description: It finally happened. The Bucks are trading Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis to the Heat for Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis, three first-round picks (including No. 13 in the NBA Draft), one pick swap, and one second-rounder.  🚨 Julius Randle traded to Nets in 3-team deal: https://bit.ly/4w5CNPq 🍕 Join us tonight for No Dunks' 2026 NBA Draft Watch Party: https://bit.ly/4vsfUpx ✍️ No Dunks' Substack has ad-free episodes: https://nodunks.substack.com/ 👕 Fix up, look sharp in No Dunks merch: https://bit.ly/4qi6nin 📈 Subscribe to No Dunks on YouTube: YouTube.com/nodunksinc 𝕏 Follow No Dunks on Twitter/X: https://x.com/NoDunksInc 📸 Follow No Dunks on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nodunksinc/  #NBA #Giannis #MiamiHeat

items.title: June 23, 2005: The Spurs defeat the Pistons, 81-74, in Game 7 of the NBA Finals to win their third NBA championship in seven seasons.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 23, 2005: The Spurs defeat the Pistons, 81-74, in Game 7 of the NBA Finals to win their third NBA championship in seven seasons.

Finals MVP Tim Duncan finished with 25 PTS/11 REB/2 BLK and Manu Ginóbili added 23 PTS for San Antonio. Richard Hamilton led Detroit with 15 PTS.
